children.heading: Tariff modifications provided for in the Agreement
children.label: a Tariff modifications provided for in the Agreement
A
children.text: such modifications or continuation of any duty,

children.label: B
children.text: such continuation of duty-free or excise treatment, or

children.heading: Effect on GSP status
children.label: 2 Effect on GSP status
children.text: Notwithstanding section 502(a)(1) of the Trade Act of 1974 (), the President shall terminate the designation of each CAFTA–DR country as a beneficiary developing country for purposes of title V of the Trade Act of 1974 [ et seq.] on the date the Agreement enters into force with respect to that country.19 U.S.C. 2462(a)(1)19 U.S.C. 2461

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding section 212(a) of the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act (), the President shall terminate the designation of each CAFTA–DR country as a beneficiary country for purposes of that Act [ et seq.] on the date the Agreement enters into force with respect to that country.19 U.S.C. 2702(a)19 U.S.C. 2701

children.label: ii
children.text: the duty-free treatment provided under paragraph 12 of Appendix I of the General Notes to the Schedule of the United States to Annex 3.3 of the Agreement; and
such modifications or continuation of any duty,
such modifications as the United States may agree to with a CAFTA–DR country regarding the staging of any duty treatment set forth in Annex 3.3 of the Agreement,
such continuation of duty-free or excise treatment, or

children.heading: Conversion to ad valorem rates
children.label: c Conversion to ad valorem rates
children.text: For purposes of subsections (a) and (b), with respect to any good for which the base rate in the Schedule of the United States to Annex 3.3 of the Agreement is a specific or compound rate of duty, the President may substitute for the base rate an ad valorem rate that the President determines to be equivalent to the base rate.
